title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by malicious actors to identify unpatched vulnerabilities in software dependencies. Additionally, reliance on such tools without proper validation could lead to false positives or negatives.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment before integrating it into production workflows.
Educate development and security teams on the limitations of automated vulnerability scanners to avoid over-reliance.
Monitor for updates or potential misuse of the tool within your organization or supply chain.

title: OpenClaw: Workspace .env npm_execpath could influence bundled runtime dependency install
description:
### Summary

Workspace .env npm_execpath could influence bundled runtime dependency install. In affected versions, a workspace `.env` in a repository opened by a trusted operator could override the package-manager executable path used by the install helper.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separa

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53846.

title: OpenClaw: Linux and macOS exec allowlists skipped configured argument patterns
description:
### Summary

OpenClaw's exec allowlist supported optional `argPattern` entries to restrict the arguments accepted for an allowlisted executable. In affected releases, Linux and macOS gateways skipped `argPattern` checks and treated a matching executable path as sufficient to satisfy the allowlist.

This meant an operator could configure an allowlist entry that appeared to permit only a narrow argv shape, but OpenClaw would allow other argv for the same executable without an approval prompt when

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows bypassing argument pattern restrictions for allowlisted executables, potentially enabling unauthorized or malicious command execution. This could lead to privilege escalation, data exfiltration, or system compromise on Linux and macOS systems.
recommended_actions: Update OpenClaw to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-53853.
Audit existing allowlist configurations to identify and mitigate any potential misuse of argument patterns.
Implement additional monitoring and logging for suspicious executable and argument usage to detect potential exploitation.

title: OpenClaw: BlueBubbles sender policy could match mutable conversation identifiers
description:
### Summary

BlueBubbles sender policy could match mutable conversation identifiers. In affected versions, a participant able to influence conversation-level identifiers could match an allowlist entry through conversation metadata rather than a stable sender identity.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unle

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53860.

title: OpenClaw: memory-wiki shared search could miss session visibility checks
description:
### Summary

memory-wiki shared search could miss session visibility checks. In affected versions, a caller able to search shared memory could skip the session visibility guard on the affected search path.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, allowlist, sandbox, or auth bou

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OpenClaw's memory-wiki shared search feature could allow unauthorized access to sensitive session data by bypassing visibility checks. This could lead to data leakage or unauthorized actions within the system.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update provided by the OpenClaw development team to address CVE-2026-53844.
Review and audit current configurations to ensure proper session visibility checks are enforced.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect any unauthorized access attempts exploiting this vulnerability.

title: OpenClaw: Config recovery could restore openclaw.json with broad file permissions
description:
### Summary

Config recovery could restore openclaw.json with broad file permissions. In affected versions, a local recovery path after configuration repair could leave the restored config file more readable than intended.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, allowlist, san

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53856.

title: OpenClaw: Zalo allowFrom could bind to mutable display names
description:
### Summary

Zalo allowFrom could bind to mutable display names. In affected versions, a Zalo friend or contact with mutable display metadata could match a policy entry through mutable display metadata.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, allowlist, sandbox, or auth bounda

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53857.

title: OpenClaw: Skill-command dispatch could skip before-tool-call hooks
description:
### Summary

Skill-command dispatch could skip before-tool-call hooks. In affected versions, a skill command routed through the affected dispatch path could run without the same `runBeforeToolCallHook` coverage as other tool entry points.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53845.

title: OpenClaw: Active Memory write scope could mutate global config
description:
### Summary

Active Memory write scope could mutate global config. In affected versions, a Gateway caller with `operator.write` access to the affected command could change global configuration without requiring `operator.admin`.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, allowlis

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a Gateway caller with 'operator.write' access to modify global configurations without requiring 'operator.admin' privileges. This could lead to unauthorized configuration changes, potentially disrupting system operations or introducing security weaknesses.
recommended_actions: Restrict 'operator.write' access to trusted users and review access control policies.
Apply patches or updates provided by OpenClaw to mitigate the vulnerability.
Monitor and audit global configuration changes to detect unauthorized modifications.

title: OpenClaw: Exported session HTML could keep unsafe markdown links
description:
### Summary

Exported session HTML could keep unsafe markdown links. In affected versions, content rendered into an exported session could preserve unsafe `javascript:` or `data:` links in generated HTML.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, allowlist, sandbox, or auth boun

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53841.

title: OpenClaw: Slack reaction events could ignore reaction notification settings
description:
### Summary

Slack reaction events could ignore reaction notification settings. In affected versions, a Slack reaction event delivered to the configured app could enter the agent pipeline even when reaction notifications were disabled.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53851.

title: OpenClaw: Bootstrap token replay could widen pending pairing scopes
description:
### Summary

Bootstrap token replay could widen pending pairing scopes. In affected versions, a caller with access to a pending bootstrap token could reuse the token before approval with a broader requested scope set.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, approval, allowlist, sandbox,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker with access to a pending bootstrap token to reuse it with a broader scope before approval, potentially escalating privileges or accessing unauthorized resources. However, the threat is mitigated by the trusted-operator model and specific configuration requirements.
recommended_actions: Restrict access to bootstrap tokens and ensure they are securely stored.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unauthorized token reuse attempts.
Apply available patches or updates to the affected versions of OpenClaw to mitigate the vulnerability.

title: OpenClaw: Shell positional parameters could weaken strict inline-eval checks
description:
### Summary

Shell positional parameters could weaken strict inline-eval checks. In affected versions, a command request that combines allowlisted tools with shell positional arguments could place inline-eval content in a shell carrier not covered by the strict check.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unle

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OpenClaw's handling of shell positional parameters could allow attackers to bypass strict inline-eval checks, potentially leading to unauthorized code execution or privilege escalation. This could compromise system integrity and expose sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of OpenClaw that addresses CVE-2026-53855.
Restrict the use of inline-eval commands and review configurations to ensure they do not rely on vulnerable positional parameter handling.
Conduct a security audit of systems using OpenClaw to identify and mitigate any potential exploitation vectors.

title: OpenClaw: Hostname checks could treat trailing-dot hosts inconsistently
description:
### Summary

Hostname checks could treat trailing-dot hosts inconsistently. In affected versions, a request path that accepts model- or workspace-derived URLs could present the same hostname with a trailing dot and avoid a blocklist comparison.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate policy, ap

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53859.

title: OpenClaw: Exec allowlist could miss side effects from transparent command wrappers
description:
### Summary

Exec allowlist could miss side effects from transparent command wrappers. In affected versions, a command request that reaches the exec allowlist path could be evaluated against the inner command while the wrapper invocation still executed.

This advisory is scoped to the named feature and configuration. It does not change OpenClaw's trusted-operator model: authenticated Gateway operators, installed plugins, and intentional local execution surfaces remain trusted unless a separate p

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows transparent command wrappers to bypass the exec allowlist, potentially leading to unintended command execution. However, the impact is limited to specific configurations and trusted environments, reducing the overall severity.
recommended_actions: Review and update the exec allowlist configuration to account for transparent command wrappers.
Monitor and audit command execution logs to detect any unusual or unauthorized activity.
Apply patches or updates provided by the OpenClaw maintainers to mitigate the issue.

title: JTL Shop versions 5.2.0 through 5.7.1 contains a server-side template injection vulnerability...
description: JTL Shop versions 5.2.0 through 5.7.1 contains a server-side template injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious template syntax due to unsanitized user-supplied input passed to the Smarty template engine. Attackers can exploit this flaw to read sensitive server-side values such as database credentials and encryption keys, and on versions 5.4.0 through 5.7.1, leverage registered Smarty modifiers including unserialize and file_get_contents to write a webshel

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54390.

title: Webmin allows unauthenticated attackers to read the contents of any file ending in .conf within...
description: Webmin allows unauthenticated attackers to read the contents of any file ending in .conf within module directories, due to a bypassable regex pattern.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Webmin allows unauthenticated attackers to read sensitive configuration files ending in .conf within module directories. This could expose critical system configurations, including credentials, API keys, or other sensitive data, potentially leading to further exploitation or unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Webmin to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-56021.
Restrict access to Webmin using firewall rules or IP whitelisting to limit exposure to trusted networks.
Audit and monitor access logs for any unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activity targeting .conf files.

title: Webmin accepts basic authentication without session cookies when an attacker provides the 'User...
description: Webmin accepts basic authentication without session cookies when an attacker provides the 'User-Agent: webmin' header, allowing bypass of additional MFA requirements. Fixed in 2.641.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56022.

title: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.468 contains a resource exhaustion vulnerability in the unauthenticated...
description: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.468 contains a resource exhaustion vulnerability in the unauthenticated POST /api/onboarding/oauth/start endpoint that allows unbounded accumulation of in-memory flow state and daemon threads. Attackers can send repeated or concurrent requests to exhaust server memory and thread resources, potentially triggering repeated outbound device-code requests to upstream OAuth providers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-55205.

title: InHand Networks IR912 V1.0.0.r20042 and IR915 V1.0.0.r20042 (including earlier versions) were...
description: InHand Networks IR912 V1.0.0.r20042 and IR915 V1.0.0.r20042 (including earlier versions) were discovered to contain a buffer overflow vulnerability in the device registration function. This vulnerability could allow an attacker to cause a denial of service attack on the remote target device.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-38718.

title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in Saad Iqbal WP EasyPay allows Cross Site...
description:
Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in Saad Iqbal WP EasyPay allows Cross Site Request Forgery.

This issue affects WP EasyPay: from n/a through 4.4.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-56024.

title: CVE-2026-41001 - Spring Boot's ArtemisEmbeddedConfigurationFactory uses a fixed, static path for the embedded Artemis
description:
Spring Boot's ArtemisEmbeddedConfigurationFactory uses a fixed, static path for the embedded Artemis message broker's data directory when no explicit path is configured. A local attacker on the same host can pre-create this predictable directory or place a symlink before the application starts.

Affected versions:
Spring Boot 4.0.0 through 4.0.6; 3.5.0 through 3.5.14; 3.4.0 through 3.4.16; 3.3.0 through 3.3.19; 2.7.0 through 2.7.33.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a local attacker to exploit a predictable static path for the embedded Artemis message broker's data directory. This could lead to unauthorized access, data manipulation, or denial of service by pre-creating the directory or using a symlink to redirect data operations to unintended locations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Spring Boot that resolves CVE-2026-41001. Monitor the official Spring Boot release notes for the latest updates.
Manually configure a unique and secure path for the Artemis message broker's data directory to avoid reliance on the default static path.
Implement file system monitoring to detect and alert on unauthorized directory or symlink creation in sensitive application directories.

title: CVE-2026-41000 - Wss4jSecurityInterceptor did not consistently wire Apache WSS4J ReplayCache instances into RequestDa
description:
Wss4jSecurityInterceptor did not consistently wire Apache WSS4J ReplayCache instances into RequestData for validation-time checks. As a result, protections against replay of UsernameToken nonces and creation timestamps, Timestamp elements, and certain SAML one-time-use semantics could be ineffective even when operators configured a replay cache on the interceptor.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Wss4jSecurityInterceptor could allow attackers to bypass replay protections, potentially enabling replay attacks on authentication tokens (e.g., UsernameToken nonces, SAML tokens). This could lead to unauthorized access or session hijacking in systems relying on affected Spring Web Services versions.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Spring Web Services that resolves CVE-2026-41000.
Review and validate the configuration of ReplayCache instances to ensure proper implementation and functionality.
Implement additional monitoring for replay attack patterns in authentication mechanisms as a temporary mitigation.

title: CVE-2026-40999 - When WS-Addressing is used with non-anonymous ReplyTo or FaultTo addresses, Spring WS may initiate o
description:
When WS-Addressing is used with non-anonymous ReplyTo or FaultTo addresses, Spring WS may initiate outbound connections through configured WebServiceMessageSender instances to destinations taken directly from request headers without verifying that those destinations are safe to connect to.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40999.

title: CVE-2026-40998 - Jaxp13XPathTemplate evaluated XPath expressions for StreamSource and SAXSource inputs using a code p
description:
Jaxp13XPathTemplate evaluated XPath expressions for StreamSource and SAXSource inputs using a code path that parsed attacker-controlled XML with the JDK's default DocumentBuilderFactory behavior instead of Spring's hardened parser configuration. Applications that evaluate XPath against untrusted XML payloads could therefore be exposed to XML External Entity (XXE) style attacks.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40998.

title: CVE-2026-40997 - Several Spring WS integration paths with Spring Security could surface detailed account state (for e
description:
Several Spring WS integration paths with Spring Security could surface detailed account state (for example locked or disabled user semantics) to remote SOAP clients through exception messages or callback outcomes, instead of failing with generic authentication errors. That behavior assists remote attackers in distinguishing valid accounts from invalid ones and inferring lifecycle state.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40997.

title: CVE-2026-40996 - Wss4jSecurityInterceptor defaulted allowRSA15KeyTransportAlgorithm to true, overriding Apache WSS4J'
description:
Wss4jSecurityInterceptor defaulted allowRSA15KeyTransportAlgorithm to true, overriding Apache WSS4J's safer default for validation RequestData. Inbound WS-Security decryption could therefore accept RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 (rsa-1_5) encrypted key material unless operators explicitly reconfigured the flag.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40996.

title: CVE-2026-40995 - X509AuthenticationProvider could issue a fully authenticated X509AuthenticationToken when a presente
description:
X509AuthenticationProvider could issue a fully authenticated X509AuthenticationToken when a presented certificate mapped to UserDetails, without applying Spring Security's standard account lifecycle checks (disabled, locked, expired, or credentials-expired accounts).

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40995.

title: CVE-2026-40994 - Wss4jSecurityInterceptor initialized its BSP (WS-I Basic Security Profile) compliance flag so that i
description:
Wss4jSecurityInterceptor initialized its BSP (WS-I Basic Security Profile) compliance flag so that inbound validation disabled WSS4J BSP enforcement on RequestData. Services that validate WS-Security on the network could therefore accept messages that violate BSP rules, weakening protocol-level checks.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Services 5.0.0 through 5.0.1; 4.1.0 through 4.1.3; 4.0.0 through 4.0.18; 3.1.0 through 3.1.8.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40994.

title: CVE-2026-40992 - Spring Boot's Mail auto-configuration does not enable hostname verification. Applications that set t
description:
Spring Boot's Mail auto-configuration does not enable hostname verification. Applications that set the relevant JavaMail property, such as spring.mail.properties.mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity=true, are not affected.

Affected versions:
Spring Boot 4.0.0 through 4.0.6; 3.5.0 through 3.5.14; 3.4.0 through 3.4.16.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40992.

title: CVE-2026-40987 - A malicious or compromised FTP/SFTP/SMB server can write arbitrary files anywhere on the client file
description:
A malicious or compromised FTP/SFTP/SMB server can write arbitrary files anywhere on the client filesystem (outside the configured local-directory) with attacker-controlled content.

Affected versions:
Spring Integration 7.0.0 through 7.0.4; 6.5.0 through 6.5.8; 6.4.0 through 6.4.11; 6.3.0 through 6.3.14; 5.5.0 through 5.5.20.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40987.

title: CVE-2026-40986 - Spring Web Flow's JavaScript RemotingHandler renders the body of an error response as HTML even when
description:
Spring Web Flow's JavaScript RemotingHandler renders the body of an error response as HTML even when the response is not "text/html", which can result in a scripting attack in the user's browser if the error response from the server contains error details with input reflected from an attacker.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Flow 4.0.0; 3.0.0 through 3.0.1; 2.5.0 through 2.5.1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40986.

title: CVE-2026-10795 - The UpdraftPlus: WP Backup & Migration Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication B
description: The UpdraftPlus: WP Backup & Migration Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass in all versions up to, and including, 1.26.4 via the UpdraftPlus_Remote_Communications_V2::wp_loaded function. This is due to insufficient validation of the remote communications message format, where signature verification can be bypassed and unchecked decryption return values collapse to a predictable all-zero encryption key. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to forge arbitrary RPC commands and run them as the connected administrator, such as uploading and activating a malicious plugin, which ultimately leads to remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10795.

title: CVE-2026-40985 - Applications that configure the WebFlowELExpressionParser are vulnerable to the use of malicious Uni
description:
Applications that configure the WebFlowELExpressionParser are vulnerable to the use of malicious Unified EL expressions.

Affected versions:
Spring Web Flow 4.0.0; 3.0.0 through 3.0.1; 2.5.0 through 2.5.1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40985.

title: CVE-2026-35273 - Vulnerability in the PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools product of Oracle PeopleSoft (component: Upda
description: Vulnerability in the PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools product of Oracle PeopleSoft (component: Updates Environment Management). Supported versions that are affected are 8.61 and 8.62. Easily exploitable vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTP to compromise PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools. Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in takeover of PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools. CVSS 3.1 Base Score 9.8 (Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability impacts). CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35273.

title: CVE-2026-2827 - The Open User Map PRO plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'oum
description: The Open User Map PRO plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'oum_location_notification' parameter in versions up to, and including, 1.4.31 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2827.
